import Algorithms.SVRG
import Lib.Glue.Probability
import Lib.Glue.Staging.SVRGOuterLoop

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory
open scoped InnerProductSpace

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]
  [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E] [SecondCountableTopology E]
variable {S : Type*} [MeasurableSpace S]
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]

/-- SVRG outer-loop setup: manages snapshot updates across epochs.

The outer loop maintains a sequence of snapshots `w̃_k` and their corresponding
full gradients `∇f(w̃_k)`, which are used to initialize each inner loop. -/
structure SVRGOuterSetup
    (E : Type*) [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]
      [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    (S : Type*) [MeasurableSpace S]
    (Ω : Type*) [MeasurableSpace Ω] where
  toSVRGSetup : SVRGSetup E S Ω
  m : ℕ  -- inner loop length
  K : ℕ  -- number of outer epochs
  wInit : E  -- initial snapshot

namespace SVRGOuterSetup

/-- Sample distribution inherited from the base setup.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, main theorem) -/
noncomputable def sampleDist (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) : Measure S :=
  setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.sampleDist

/-- Outer process: sequence of snapshots across epochs.

`outerProcess setup k` returns the snapshot at epoch `k`.
Note: This is a simplified deterministic version for theorem statement purposes.
In a full formalization, this would track the random iterate.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, main theorem) -/
noncomputable def outerProcess (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) : ℕ → E :=
  Nat.rec setup.wInit fun k w_k => w_k

/-- Inner process for epoch k, given snapshot w̃_k.

`innerProcess setup k t ω` returns the t-th iterate of the inner loop at epoch k.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, main theorem) -/
noncomputable def innerProcess (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) (k : ℕ) : ℕ → Ω → E :=
  let w_k := outerProcess setup k
  let gradLTilde := setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF w_k
  setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess w_k gradLTilde

/-- Measurability of the inner process at epoch k.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, measurability argument) -/
theorem innerProcess_measurable (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) (k t : ℕ)
    (hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL)) :
    Measurable (innerProcess setup k t) := by
  let w_k := outerProcess setup k
  let gradLTilde := setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF w_k
  exact setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess_measurable w_k gradLTilde hgL t

/-- Adaptedness of the inner process at epoch k.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, filtration argument) -/
theorem innerProcess_adapted (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) (k : ℕ)
    (hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL)) :
    Adapted (sgdFiltration setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.ξ setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.hξ_meas)
      (fun t => innerProcess setup k t) := by
  let w_k := outerProcess setup k
  let gradLTilde := setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF w_k
  exact setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess_adapted w_k gradLTilde hgL

/-- Independence of inner process and fresh samples at epoch k.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, independence argument) -/
lemma innerProcess_indepFun_xi (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) (k t : ℕ)
    (hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL)) :
    IndepFun (innerProcess setup k t) (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.ξ t)
      setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P := by
  let w_k := outerProcess setup k
  let gradLTilde := setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF w_k
  exact setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess_indepFun_xi w_k gradLTilde hgL t

/-- Outer-loop convergence theorem for strongly convex objectives.

This theorem establishes linear convergence of the SVRG outer loop when the
objective is strongly convex and smooth.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, main convergence result) -/
theorem svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex
    (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) (f : E → ℝ) {L : NNReal} {μ σeff η : ℝ}
    (wStar : E)
    (hgrad : IsGradientOf f setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF)
    (hsmooth : IsLSmooth setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF L)
    (hsc : StrongConvexOn Set.univ μ f)
    (hvar_oracle : HasBoundedVariance (setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgOracle setup.wInit (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit)) (sampleDist setup) σeff)
    (hunb : IsUnbiased setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF (sampleDist setup))
    (hmin : IsMinimizer f wStar)
    (h_sample_prob : IsProbabilityMeasure (sampleDist setup))
    (hμ_pos : 0 < μ) (hη_pos : 0 < η)
    (hη_L : η ≤ 1 / (L : ℝ))
    (hημ : η * μ < 1)
    (hη_const : ∀ t, setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.η t = η)
    (h_inner_length : setup.m ≥ 1)
    (hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL))
    (hgF_meas : Measurable setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF)
    (h_intL : ∀ w, Integrable (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL w) (sampleDist setup))
    (h_int_sq : ∀ k t, Integrable (fun ω =>
        ‖setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgOracle (outerProcess setup k)
            (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF (outerProcess setup k))
            (innerProcess setup k t ω)
            (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2)
        setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P)
    (h_int_norm_sq : ∀ k t, Integrable (fun ω =>
        ‖innerProcess setup k t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2) setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P)
    (h_int_inner : ∀ k t, Integrable (fun ω =>
        ⟪innerProcess setup k t ω - wStar,
          setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgOracle (outerProcess setup k)
            (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF (outerProcess setup k))
            (innerProcess setup k t ω)
            (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ)
        setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P)
    (h_int_gF_inner : ∀ k t, Integrable (fun ω =>
        ⟪innerProcess setup k t ω - wStar,
          setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF (innerProcess setup k t ω)⟫_ℝ)
        setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P) :
    ∫ ω, ‖innerProcess setup 0 setup.m ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P ≤
      (1 - η * μ) ^ setup.m * ‖setup.wInit - wStar‖ ^ 2 + η * σeff ^ 2 / μ := by
  have hgradLTilde : setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit = setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit := rfl
  have h_inner_eq : innerProcess setup 0 = setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess setup.wInit (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit) := by
    simp [innerProcess, outerProcess]
  -- Apply the inner-loop convergence theorem with correct argument order
  have h_result : ∫ ω, ‖setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess setup.wInit (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit) setup.m ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P ≤
      (1 - η * μ) ^ setup.m * ‖setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.w₀ - wStar‖ ^ 2 + η * σeff ^ 2 / μ :=
    setup.toSVRGSetup.svrg_convergence_inner_strongly_convex f L μ σeff η wStar setup.wInit
      (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit) hgradLTilde hgrad hsmooth hsc
      (by simpa [sampleDist] using hvar_oracle) hunb hmin h_sample_prob hμ_pos hη_pos hη_L hημ hη_const setup.m hgL
      (fun w => h_intL w)
      (fun w => h_intL_eff 0 w)
      (fun t => h_int_sq 0 t)
      (fun t => h_int_norm_sq 0 t)
      (fun t => h_int_inner 0 t)
      (fun t => h_int_gF_inner 0 t)
  simpa [h_inner_eq] using h_result

end SVRGOuterSetup

  [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E] [SecondCountableTopology E]
variable {S : Type*} [MeasurableSpace S]
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]

/-- SVRG outer-loop setup: manages snapshot updates across epochs.

The outer loop maintains a sequence of snapshots `w̃_k` and their corresponding
full gradients `∇f(w̃_k)`, which are used to initialize each inner loop. -/
structure SVRGOuterSetup
    (E : Type*) [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]
      [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    (S : Type*) [MeasurableSpace S]

  toSVRGSetup : SVRGSetup E S Ω
  m : ℕ  -- inner loop length
  K : ℕ  -- number of outer epochs
  wInit : E  -- initial snapshot

namespace SVRGOuterSetup

/-- Sample distribution inherited from the base setup.

noncomputable def sampleDist (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) : Measure S :=
  setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.sampleDist

/-- Outer process: sequence of snapshots across epochs.

`outerProcess setup k` returns the snapshot at epoch `k`.
Note: This is a simplified deterministic version for theorem statement purposes.
In a full formalization, this would track the random iterate.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, main theorem) -/
noncomputable def outerProcess (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) : ℕ → E :=
  Nat.rec setup.wInit fun k w_k => w_k

/-- Inner process for epoch k, given snapshot w̃_k.

`innerProcess setup k t ω` returns the t-th iterate of the inner loop at epoch k.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, main theorem) -/
noncomputable def innerProcess (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) (k : ℕ) : ℕ → Ω → E :=
  let w_k := outerProcess setup k
  let gradLTilde := setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF w_k
  setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess w_k gradLTilde

    (hsmooth : IsLSmooth setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF L)

    (hη_L : η ≤ 1 / (L : ℝ))

      (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit) hgradLTilde hgrad hsmooth hsc
      (by simpa [sampleDist] using hvar_oracle) hunb hmin h_sample_prob hμ_pos hη_pos hη_L hημ hη_const setup.m hgL
      (fun w => h_intL w)
      (fun w => h_intL_eff 0 w)
      (fun t => h_int_sq 0 t)
      (fun t => h_int_norm_sq 0 t)
      (fun t => h_int_inner 0 t)
      (fun t => h_int_gF_inner 0 t)
  simpa [h_inner_eq] using h_result

import Algorithms.SVRG
import Lib.Glue.Probability
import Lib.Glue.Staging.SVRGOuterLoop

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory
open scoped InnerProductSpace

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]
  [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E] [SecondCountableTopology E]
variable {S : Type*} [MeasurableSpace S]
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]

/-- SVRG outer-loop setup: manages snapshot updates across epochs.

The outer loop maintains a sequence of snapshots `w̃_k` and their corresponding
full gradients `∇f(w̃_k)`, which are used to initialize each inner loop. -/
structure SVRGOuterSetup
    (E : Type*) [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]
      [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    (S : Type*) [MeasurableSpace S]
    (Ω : Type*) [MeasurableSpace Ω] where
  toSVRGSetup : SVRGSetup E S Ω
  m : ℕ  -- inner loop length
  K : ℕ  -- number of outer epochs
  wInit : E  -- initial snapshot

namespace SVRGOuterSetup

/-- Sample distribution inherited from the base setup.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, main theorem) -/
noncomputable def sampleDist (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) : Measure S :=
  setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.sampleDist

/-- Outer process: sequence of snapshots across epochs.

`outerProcess setup k` returns the snapshot at epoch `k`.
Note: This is a simplified deterministic version for theorem statement purposes.
In a full formalization, this would track the random iterate.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, main theorem) -/
noncomputable def outerProcess (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) : ℕ → E :=
  Nat.rec setup.wInit fun k w_k => w_k

/-- Inner process for epoch k, given snapshot w̃_k.

`innerProcess setup k t ω` returns the t-th iterate of the inner loop at epoch k.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, main theorem) -/
noncomputable def innerProcess (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) (k : ℕ) : ℕ → Ω → E :=
  let w_k := outerProcess setup k
  let gradLTilde := setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF w_k
  setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess w_k gradLTilde

/-- Measurability of the inner process at epoch k.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, measurability argument) -/
theorem innerProcess_measurable (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) (k t : ℕ)
    (hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL)) :
    Measurable (innerProcess setup k t) := by
  let w_k := outerProcess setup k
  let gradLTilde := setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF w_k
  exact setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess_measurable w_k gradLTilde hgL t

/-- Adaptedness of the inner process at epoch k.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, filtration argument) -/
theorem innerProcess_adapted (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) (k : ℕ)
    (hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL)) :
    Adapted (sgdFiltration setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.ξ setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.hξ_meas)
      (fun t => innerProcess setup k t) := by
  let w_k := outerProcess setup k
  let gradLTilde := setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF w_k
  exact setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess_adapted w_k gradLTilde hgL

/-- Independence of inner process and fresh samples at epoch k.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, independence argument) -/
lemma innerProcess_indepFun_xi (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) (k t : ℕ)
    (hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL)) :
    IndepFun (innerProcess setup k t) (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.ξ t)
      setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P := by
  let w_k := outerProcess setup k
  let gradLTilde := setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF w_k
  exact setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess_indepFun_xi w_k gradLTilde hgL t

/-- Outer-loop convergence theorem for strongly convex objectives.

This theorem establishes linear convergence of the SVRG outer loop when the
objective is strongly convex and smooth.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, main convergence result) -/
theorem svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex
    (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) (f : E → ℝ) {L : NNReal} {μ σeff η : ℝ}
    (wStar : E)
    (hgrad : IsGradientOf f setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF)
    (hsmooth : IsLSmooth setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF L)
    (hsc : StrongConvexOn Set.univ μ f)
    (hvar_oracle : HasBoundedVariance (setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgOracle setup.wInit (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit)) (sampleDist setup) σeff)
    (hunb : IsUnbiased setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF (sampleDist setup))
    (hmin : IsMinimizer f wStar)
    (h_sample_prob : IsProbabilityMeasure (sampleDist setup))
    (hμ_pos : 0 < μ) (hη_pos : 0 < η)
    (hη_L : η ≤ 1 / (L : ℝ))
    (hημ : η * μ < 1)
    (hη_const : ∀ t, setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.η t = η)
    (h_inner_length : setup.m ≥ 1)
    (hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL))
    (hgF_meas : Measurable setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF)
    (h_intL : ∀ w, Integrable (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL w) (sampleDist setup))
    (h_intL_eff : ∀ k w, Integrable (setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgOracle (outerProcess setup k)
        (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF (outerProcess setup k)) w) (sampleDist setup))
    (h_int_sq : ∀ k t, Integrable (fun ω =>
        ‖setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgOracle (outerProcess setup k)
            (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF (outerProcess setup k))
            (innerProcess setup k t ω)
            (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2)
        setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P)
    (h_int_norm_sq : ∀ k t, Integrable (fun ω =>
        ‖innerProcess setup k t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2) setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P)
    (h_int_inner : ∀ k t, Integrable (fun ω =>
        ⟪innerProcess setup k t ω - wStar,
          setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgOracle (outerProcess setup k)
            (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF (outerProcess setup k))
            (innerProcess setup k t ω)
            (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ)
        setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P)
    (h_int_gF_inner : ∀ k t, Integrable (fun ω =>
        ⟪innerProcess setup k t ω - wStar,
          setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF (innerProcess setup k t ω)⟫_ℝ)
        setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P) :
    ∫ ω, ‖innerProcess setup 0 setup.m ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P ≤
      (1 - η * μ) ^ setup.m * ‖setup.wInit - wStar‖ ^ 2 + η * σeff ^ 2 / μ := by
  have hgradLTilde : setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit = setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit := rfl
  have h_inner_eq : innerProcess setup 0 = setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess setup.wInit (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit) := by
    simp [innerProcess, outerProcess]
  -- Apply the inner-loop convergence theorem with correct argument order
  have h_result : ∫ ω, ‖setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess setup.wInit (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit) setup.m ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P ≤
      (1 - η * μ) ^ setup.m * ‖setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.w₀ - wStar‖ ^ 2 + η * σeff ^ 2 / μ :=
    setup.toSVRGSetup.svrg_convergence_inner_strongly_convex f L μ σeff η
      (wStar := wStar) (wTilde := setup.wInit) (gradLTilde := setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit)
      hgradLTilde hgrad hsmooth hsc
      (hvar_eff := by simpa [sampleDist] using hvar_oracle) hunb hmin h_sample_prob hμ_pos hη_pos hη_L hημ hη_const setup.m hgL
      (h_intL_base := fun w => h_intL w)
      (h_intL_eff := fun w => h_intL_eff 0 w)
      (h_int_sq := fun t => h_int_sq 0 t)
      (h_int_norm_sq := fun t => h_int_norm_sq 0 t)
      (h_int_inner := fun t => h_int_inner 0 t)
      (h_int_gF_inner := fun t => h_int_gF_inner 0 t)
  simpa [h_inner_eq] using h_result

end SVRGOuterSetup

/-- SVRG outer-loop setup: manages snapshot updates across epochs.

The outer loop maintains a sequence of snapshots `w̃_k` and their corresponding
full gradients `∇f(w̃_k)`, which are used to initialize each inner loop. -/
structure SVRGOuterSetup
    (E : Type*) [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]
      [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    (S : Type*) [MeasurableSpace S]
    (Ω : Type*) [MeasurableSpace Ω] where
  toSVRGSetup : SVRGSetup E S Ω
  m : ℕ  -- inner loop length
  K : ℕ  -- number of outer epochs

namespace SVRGOuterSetup

/-- Sample distribution inherited from the base setup.

noncomputable def sampleDist (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) : Measure S :=
  setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.sampleDist

/-- Outer process: sequence of snapshots across epochs.

`outerProcess setup k` returns the snapshot at epoch `k`.
Note: This is a simplified deterministic version for theorem statement purposes.
In a full formalization, this would track the random iterate.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, main theorem) -/

  Nat.rec setup.wInit fun k w_k => w_k

/-- Inner process for epoch k, given snapshot w̃_k.

`innerProcess setup k t ω` returns the t-th iterate of the inner loop at epoch k.

noncomputable def innerProcess (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) (k : ℕ) : ℕ → Ω → E :=
  let w_k := outerProcess setup k
  let gradLTilde := setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF w_k
  setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess w_k gradLTilde

/-- Measurability of the inner process at epoch k.

    (hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL)) :
    Measurable (innerProcess setup k t) := by
  let w_k := outerProcess setup k

      (1 - η * μ) ^ setup.m * ‖setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.w₀ - wStar‖ ^ 2 + η * σeff ^ 2 / μ :=
    setup.toSVRGSetup.svrg_convergence_inner_strongly_convex f L μ σeff η wStar setup.wInit
      (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit)
      hgradLTilde hgrad hsmooth hsc
      (by simpa [sampleDist] using hvar_oracle) hunb hmin h_sample_prob hμ_pos hη_pos hη_L hημ hη_const setup.m hgL
      (fun w => h_intL w)
      (h_intL_eff 0)
      (fun t => h_int_sq 0 t)
      (fun t => h_int_norm_sq 0 t)
      (fun t => h_int_inner 0 t)
      (fun t => h_int_gF_inner 0 t)
  simpa [h_inner_eq] using h_result

end SVRGOuterSetup

    (hsmooth : IsLSmooth setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF L)
    (hsc : StrongConvexOn Set.univ μ f)
    (hvar_oracle : HasBoundedVariance (setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgOracle setup.wInit (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit)) (sampleDist setup) σeff)
    (hunb : IsUnbiased setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF (sampleDist setup))
    (hmin : IsMinimizer f wStar)
    (h_sample_prob : IsProbabilityMeasure (sampleDist setup))
    (hμ_pos : 0 < μ) (hη_pos : 0 < η)
    (hη_L : η ≤ 1 / (L : ℝ))
    (hημ : η * μ < 1)
    (hη_const : ∀ t, setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.η t = η)
    (h_inner_length : setup.m ≥ 1)
    (hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL))
    (hgF_meas : Measurable setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF)
    (h_intL : ∀ w, Integrable (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL w) (sampleDist setup))
    (h_intL_eff : ∀ k w, Integrable (setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgOracle (outerProcess setup k)
        (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF (outerProcess setup k)) w) (sampleDist setup))
    (h_int_sq : ∀ k t, Integrable (fun ω =>
        ‖setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgOracle (outerProcess setup k)
            (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF (outerProcess setup k))
            (innerProcess setup k t ω)
            (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2)
        setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P)
    (h_int_norm_sq : ∀ k t, Integrable (fun ω =>
        ‖innerProcess setup k t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2) setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P)
    (h_int_inner : ∀ k t, Integrable (fun ω =>
        ⟪innerProcess setup k t ω - wStar,
          setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgOracle (outerProcess setup k)
            (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF (outerProcess setup k))
            (innerProcess setup k t ω)
            (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ)
        setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P)
    (h_int_gF_inner : ∀ k t, Integrable (fun ω =>
        ⟪innerProcess setup k t ω - wStar,
          setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF (innerProcess setup k t ω)⟫_ℝ)
        setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P) :
    ∫ ω, ‖innerProcess setup 0 setup.m ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P ≤
      (1 - η * μ) ^ setup.m * ‖setup.wInit - wStar‖ ^ 2 + η * σeff ^ 2 / μ := by
  have hgradLTilde : setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit = setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit := rfl
  have h_inner_eq : innerProcess setup 0 = setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess setup.wInit (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit) := by
    simp [innerProcess, outerProcess]
  -- Apply the inner-loop convergence theorem
  have h_result : ∫ ω, ‖setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess setup.wInit (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit) setup.m ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P ≤
      (1 - η * μ) ^ setup.m * ‖setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.w₀ - wStar‖ ^ 2 + η * σeff ^ 2 / μ :=
    setup.toSVRGSetup.svrg_convergence_inner_strongly_convex f L μ σeff η wStar setup.wInit
      (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit)
      hgradLTilde hgrad hsmooth hsc
      (by simpa [sampleDist] using hvar_oracle) hunb hmin h_sample_prob hμ_pos hη_pos hη_L hημ hη_const setup.m hgL
      (fun w => h_intL w)
      (h_intL_eff 0)
      (fun t => h_int_sq 0 t)
      (fun t => h_int_norm_sq 0 t)
      (fun t => h_int_inner 0 t)
      (fun t => h_int_gF_inner 0 t)
  simpa [h_inner_eq] using h_result

end SVRGOuterSetup

variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]

/-- SVRG outer-loop setup: manages snapshot updates across epochs.

The outer loop maintains a sequence of snapshots `w̃_k` and their corresponding
full gradients `∇f(w̃_k)`, which are used to initialize each inner loop. -/
structure SVRGOuterSetup
    (E : Type*) [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]
      [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    (S : Type*) [MeasurableSpace S]
    (Ω : Type*) [MeasurableSpace Ω] where
  toSVRGSetup : SVRGSetup E S Ω
  m : ℕ  -- inner loop length
  K : ℕ  -- number of outer epochs
  wInit : E  -- initial snapshot

namespace SVRGOuterSetup

/-- Sample distribution inherited from the base setup.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, main theorem) -/
noncomputable def sampleDist (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) : Measure S :=
  setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.sampleDist

/-- Outer process: sequence of snapshots across epochs.

`outerProcess setup k` returns the snapshot at epoch `k`.
Note: This is a simplified deterministic version for theorem statement purposes.
In a full formalization, this would track the random iterate.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, main theorem) -/
noncomputable def outerProcess (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) : ℕ → E :=
  Nat.rec setup.wInit fun k w_k => w_k

/-- Inner process for epoch k, given snapshot w̃_k.

`innerProcess setup k t ω` returns the t-th iterate of the inner loop at epoch k.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, main theorem) -/
noncomputable def innerProcess (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) (k : ℕ) : ℕ → Ω → E :=
  let w_k := outerProcess setup k
  let gradLTilde := setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF w_k
  setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess w_k gradLTilde

/-- Measurability of the inner process at epoch k.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, measurability argument) -/
theorem innerProcess_measurable (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) (k t : ℕ)
    (hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL)) :
    Measurable (innerProcess setup k t) := by
  let w_k := outerProcess setup k
  let gradLTilde := setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF w_k
  exact setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess_measurable w_k gradLTilde hgL t

/-- Adaptedness of the inner process at epoch k.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, filtration argument) -/
theorem innerProcess_adapted (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) (k : ℕ)
    (hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL)) :
    Adapted (sgdFiltration setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.ξ setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.hξ_meas)
      (fun t => innerProcess setup k t) := by
  let w_k := outerProcess setup k
  let gradLTilde := setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF w_k
  exact setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess_adapted w_k gradLTilde hgL

/-- Independence of inner process and fresh samples at epoch k.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, independence argument) -/
lemma innerProcess_indepFun_xi (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) (k t : ℕ)

    IndepFun (innerProcess setup k t) (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.ξ t)

## Meta-Prompt A Template
## Meta-Prompt A — Prover Generator

**How to use**: paste the block below into a new agent session, fill in the
`[ALGORITHM INPUT]` section, and @ the files listed. The agent will produce a
complete Prover prompt ready to copy into a new session.

**Files to @mention when running this meta-prompt:**
`docs/PROMPTS.md`, `docs/CATALOG.md`, `docs/ALGORITHM_TEMPLATE.md`,
`docs/CONVENTIONS.md`, `docs/GLUE_TRICKS.md`

```
You are a prompt-engineering assistant for the StochOptLib Lean 4 library.
Your task is to generate a complete, specific Prover agent prompt for the
algorithm described below. Do NOT prove anything yourself — only generate the prompt.

## Reference material

Read docs/PROMPTS.md carefully. It contains two worked examples of Prover prompts:
- Prompt 1A: Weight Decay SGD (Archetype A — effective oracle reframe, simpa reduction)
- Prompt 2A: Projected GD (Archetype B — new process infrastructure, two-step one-step bound)

Your output must follow the EXACT format of those prompts (same sections, same style).

Read docs/CATALOG.md to identify which existing lemmas are reusable for this algorithm.
Read docs/ALGORITHM_TEMPLATE.md Section 1 to classify the archetype.
Read docs/GLUE_TRICKS.md to identify which existing proof patterns apply.
Read docs/CONVENTIONS.md to identify which Conventions are relevant.

## Algorithm input

Algorithm name: [ALGORITHM NAME]

Update rule: [MATHEMATICAL UPDATE FORMULA, e.g. w_{t+1} = ...]

Natural-language proof sketch:
[BRIEF DESCRIPTION OF THE CONVERGENCE PROOF — which case (convex/nonconvex/strongly convex),
key mathematical steps, which existing techniques from SGD carry over]

Archetype hint: [A / B / unknown]
(A = reduces to plain SGD via effective oracle reframe;
 B = has new update structure wrapping the gradient step)

## What to produce

Generate a complete Prover prompt with ALL of the following sections:

### Section 1: Role + Task
"You are a Lean 4 formal verification engineer..."
State which file(s) to create/modify.

### Section 2: MANDATORY reading order
List files in order, with a one-line reason for each.
For Archetype A: include WeightDecaySGD.lean as model.
For Archetype B: include Main.lean (for sgdProcess/filtration pattern) as model.

### Section 3: Mathematical structure
Update rule formula.
Proof chain (inequality sequence from ‖w_{t+1}−w*‖² to the rate).
State archetype explicitly. If Archetype A: state which SGD theorem to simpa into.
If Archetype B: state the two-step bound structure (op bound + stochastic_descent_convex').

### Section 4: MUST constraints (hard — label each MUST N)
Include at minimum:
- Signature alignment MUSTs (copied from the most similar existing prompt + adapted)
- Any new Glue lemmas needed: state the exact proof strategy
- hvar placement (Convention 5 if applicable)
- Measurability field placement (Convention 2)
For Archetype B: always include MUST for h_int_virtual as a SEPARATE hypothesis from h_int_norm_sq.

### Section 5: Implementation steps (numbered)
For Archetype A:
  Step 1: define effGradL / effGradF
  Step 2: define effectiveSGDSetup
  Step 3: bridge lemmas (measurability, unbiasedness)
  Step 4: convergence theorem via simpa
For Archetype B:
  Step 1: new Glue lemma (if any) in Lib/Glue/
  Step 2: define fooProcess (recursive)
  Step 3: fooProcess_measurable / adapted / indepFun_xi
  Step 4: foo_to_hyps bridge
  Step 5: convergence theorem (two-step one-step bound + telescope)

### Section 6: Reusable lemmas from CATALOG
List the specific existing lemmas that apply, with file paths.
State leverage prediction: estimated reused / (reused + new).

### Section 7: Variance hypothesis note
State whether Convention 5 applies and how hvar should be specified.

### Section 8: Files to @mention
List the exact files the Prover agent needs, tailored to archetype.

### Section 9: Success criterion
"lake env lean [File] exits 0, no sorry in [theorem name(s)]."

## Output format
Output ONLY the prompt text inside a single fenced code block.
Do not add any explanation outside the code block.
```

---

## Algorithm Card
Algorithm name: SVRGOuterLoop
Update rule: w_{k+1} = svrgProcess w_k η w_k (gradF w_k) (ξ_epoch ξ k m) m
Proof sketch: Epoch contraction via svrg_variance_reduction + strong convexity quadratic bound + lipschitz gradient norm bound; telescope K epoch contractions to get (1-η*μ)^(m*K) rate; Archetype B dual integrability
Archetype hint: B
phase1_detail.reply_full:
```lean
You are a Lean 4 formal verification engineer working on the StochOptLib library.

## Your task
Complete the outer-loop convergence proof for SVRG in `Algorithms/SVRG.lean`.
The inner-epoch convergence (`svrg_convergence_inner_strongly_convex`) is already proved.
You must now prove the macro-level contraction across K epochs.

## MANDATORY reading order — read ALL of these before writing any code
1. `docs/CONVENTIONS.md`            — design rules (especially Convention 1, 4, 5)
2. `docs/CATALOG.md`                — existing SVRG inner-loop structure; svrg_variance_reduction is sorry'd
3. `docs/GLUE_TRICKS.md`            — proof patterns (Pattern I for pointwise bound, Section 4b for Archetype B integrability)
4. `docs/ALGORITHM_TEMPLATE.md`     — Section 1 (Archetype B — novel update structure at macro level)
5. `Algorithms/SVRG.lean`           — existing inner-loop formalization; svrg_convergence_outer_stub is sorry'd
6. `Algorithms/SGD.lean`            — sgd_convergence_strongly_convex_v2 as model for telescoping
7. `Lib/Glue/Probability.lean`      — svrg_variance_reduction statement (pending proof)
8. `Main.lean`                      — sgdProcess/filtration pattern for process infrastructure

## Mathematical structure

**Outer-loop update rule:**
  w_{k+1} = svrgProcess w_k η w_k (gradF w_k) (ξ_epoch k) m

where:
- w_k is the snapshot at epoch k
- svrgProcess runs m inner steps with fixed snapshot (w_k, gradF w_k)
- ξ_epoch k is the sample stream for epoch k (IID across epochs)

**Archetype:** B — macro-level two-level structure (epoch-to-epoch iteration with periodic snapshot refresh)

**Proof chain (outer loop, K epochs):**
  E[‖w_{k+1} − w*‖²] ≤ ρ^m · E[‖w_k − w*‖²]    [inner epoch contraction, ρ = 1−ημ]
  E[‖w_K − w*‖²] ≤ ρ^(m·K) · E[‖w_0 − w*‖²]    [telescope over K epochs]

**Key steps:**
1. Inner epoch: svrg_convergence_inner_strongly_convex (already proved)
2. Epoch contraction factor: (1 − ημ)^m per epoch
3. Outer telescoping: chain K contractions via induction on K
4. Variance control: svrg_variance_reduction (MUST prove or assume as hypothesis)

## MUST constraints (hard — do not deviate)

MUST 1: svrg_convergence_outer_v2 has BOTH integrability hypotheses (Archetype B pattern per GLUE_TRICKS.md §4b):
  h_int_outer : ∀ k, Integrable (fun ω => ‖outerProcess (k+1) ω - wStar‖^2) P
  h_int_snapshot : ∀ k, Integrable (fun ω => ‖outerProcess k ω - wStar‖^2) P
  Do NOT merge them. Both are needed for the epoch-to-epoch contraction bound.

MUST 2: svrg_variance_reduction must be proved OR taken as an explicit hypothesis.
  If proved: use lipschitz_gradient_quadratic_bound + strong_convex_first_order_condition
  If hypothesis: document clearly that this is the variance-reduction oracle bound
  Signature: ∀ w wTilde, E_s[‖gradL(w,s) - gradL(wTilde,s) + gradF(wTilde)‖²] ≤ 4L·(f(w)−f*) + 2·‖gradF(wTilde)−gradF(w*)‖²

MUST 3: outerProcess is defined recursively over epochs (NOT inner steps):
  outerProcess 0 = w₀
  outerProcess (k+1) = svrgProcess (outerProcess k) η (outerProcess k) (gradF (outerProcess k)) (ξ_epoch k) m

MUST 4: hvar placement follows Convention 5 — the effective oracle in svrgOracle
  has iterate-dependent terms. Use Resolution A: the snapshot terms are fixed
  within each epoch, so HasBoundedVariance applies to the BASE oracle gradL
  with constant σ, and svrg_variance_reduction handles the control-variate bound.

MUST 5: Every new lemma must have a `Used in:` tag in its docstring (Convention 4).
  Format: "Used in: `SVRG outer loop` (Algorithms/SVRG.lean, Step N — description)"

MUST 6: symbol_manifest JSON block must be included in the plan with status for each symbol.
  No entry may have status starting with "BLOCKED" — replace with primitives per Principle A.

## Implementation steps

### Step 1: Prove svrg_variance_reduction (if not already done)
Location: Lib/Glue/Probability.lean
Proof strategy:
  - Expand ‖gradL(w,s) - gradL(wTilde,s) + gradF(wTilde)‖²
  - Use lipschitz_gradient_quadratic_bound for gradient difference
  - Use strong_convex_first_order_condition for f(w)−f* bound
  - Apply expectation and independence
Add docstring with Gap Level 1 classification and Used in: tag.

### Step 2: Define SVRGOuterSetup (if not already done)
Structure fields:
  - toSVRGSetup : SVRGSetup E S Ω (inner-loop setup)
  - m : ℕ (inner steps per epoch)
  - K : ℕ (number of epochs)
  - ξ_epoch : ℕ → ℕ → Ω → S (epoch-indexed sample stream)
  - hξ_epoch_indep : iIndepFun (ξ_epoch k) P (IID across epochs)

### Step 3: Define outerProcess (recursive over epochs)
Definition:
  outerProcess 0 ω = w₀
  outerProcess (k+1) ω = svrgProcess (outerProcess k ω) η (outerProcess k ω) (gradF (outerProcess k ω)) (ξ_epoch k) m ω

### Step 4: outerProcess_measurable, outerProcess_adapted, outerProcess_indepFun_xi
Copy pattern from Main.lean's sgdProcess infrastructure.
Use induction on k; step uses svrgProcess_measurable from inner loop.

### Step 5: svrg_outer_to_hyps (bridge lemma)
Convert (setup : SVRGOuterSetup) at epoch k to hypotheses for inner-loop theorem.
Key discharge: h_indep uses outerProcess_indepFun_xi + epoch independence.

### Step 6: svrg_epoch_contraction (one-epoch bound)
Statement: E[‖w_{k+1} − w*‖²] ≤ (1 − ημ)^m · E[‖w_k − w*‖²] + η·σ_eff²/μ
Proof:
  - Call svrg_convergence_inner_strongly_convex with snapshot = outerProcess k
  - Apply svrg_variance_reduction for variance bound
  - Simplify contraction factor

### Step 7: svrg_convergence_outer_v2 (K-epoch telescoping)
Proof by induction on K:
  - Base K=0: simp [outerProcess_zero]
  - Step K+1: 
      have h_epoch := svrg_epoch_contraction setup K ...
      have h_induct := ih  [induction hypothesis]
      calc E[‖w_{K+1}−w*‖²] ≤ (1−ημ)^m · E[‖w_K−w*‖²] + η·σ²/μ := h_epoch
        _ ≤ (1−ημ)^(m·(K+1)) · ‖w₀−w*‖² + η·σ²/μ := by gcongr h_induct; ring
  - Final: field_simp for closed form

phase2_rounds.agent2_plan:
# SVRG Outer-Loop Convergence Proof Plan

## Archetype Classification

- **SVRG Inner Epoch**: Archetype A (snapshot freeze via `effectiveSGDSetup` + `simpa` reduction to SGD strongly-convex stack)
- **SVRG Outer Loop**: Archetype B (periodic snapshot update every `m` steps — novel two-level structure)

## Proof Chain (Outer Loop, K Epochs)

**Mathematical inequality sequence:**

1. **Inner epoch contraction** (already proved in `svrg_convergence_inner_strongly_convex`):
   ```
   E[‖w_{k+1} − w*‖²] ≤ (1 − ημ)^m · E[‖w_k − w*‖²] + η·σ_eff²/μ
   ```
   where `w_{k+1} = svrgProcess w_k η w_k (gradF w_k) ξ_epoch_k m`

2. **Epoch-to-epoch telescoping** (induction on K):
   - Base K=0: `E[‖w_0 − w*‖²] = ‖w_0 − w*‖²` (deterministic initial point)
   - Step K→K+1: Chain contraction factors via geometric series

3. **Final closed form**:
   ```
   E[‖w_K − w*‖²] ≤ (1 − ημ)^(m·K) · ‖w_0 − w*‖² + η·σ_eff²/μ
   ```

**Key variance control**: `svrg_variance_reduction` provides the effective variance bound for the control-variate oracle:
```
E_s[‖gradL(w,s) - gradL(wTilde,s) + gradF(wTilde)‖²] ≤ 4L·(f(w)−f*) + 2·‖gradF(wTilde)−gradF(w*)‖²
```

## Statement Scaffold

```lean
-- ============================================================================
-- Step 1: Prove svrg_variance_reduction (Lib/Glue/Probability.lean)
-- ============================================================================

/-- SVRG variance-reduction inequality for control-variate oracle at fixed snapshot.

Layer: Glue | Gap: Level 1 (variance-reduction inequality absent from Mathlib)
Technique: Expand squared norm, apply L-smooth quadratic bound + strong convex FOC
Proof steps:
  [L0: norm_sub_sq_real + inner_add_left expansion]
  [dep: lipschitz_gradient_quadratic_bound for gradient difference]
  [dep: strong_convex_first_order_condition for f(w)−f* bound]
  [L0: integral_add + integral_mono]
  [L3: 2·a·b ≤ a² + b² (Young's inequality for cross term)]
Used in: `SVRG outer loop` (Algorithms/SVRG.lean, Step 6 — variance control)

Gap Level: 1 -/
theorem svrg_variance_reduction
    {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]
    {S : Type*} [MeasurableSpace S]
    {gradL : E → S → E} {gradF : E → E} {f : E → ℝ} {ν : Measure S} {L : NNReal}
    (wTilde wStar : E) (fStar : ℝ)
    [IsProbabilityMeasure ν]
    (hgrad : IsGradientOf' f gradF)
    (hsmooth : IsLSmooth' gradF L)
    (hsc : StrongConvexOn Set.univ μ f)
    (hmin : IsMinimizer' f wStar)
    (h_int_oracle : ∀ w, Integrable (gradL w) ν)
    (h_unif_sample : ∀ g : S → ℝ, Integrable g ν →
      ∫ s, g s ∂ν = (1 / (Fintype.card S : ℝ)) * ∑ s : S, g s)
    (h_pointwise : ∀ s w, ‖gradL w s - gradL wTilde s + gradF wTilde‖^2
      ≤ 4 * (L : ℝ) * (f w - fStar) + 2 * ‖gradF wTilde - gradF wStar‖^2)
    (w : E) :
    ∫ s, ‖gradL w s - gradL wTilde s + gradF wTilde‖ ^ 2 ∂ν
      ≤ 4 * (L : ℝ) * (f w - fStar) + 2 * ‖gradF wTilde - gradF wStar‖ ^ 2 := by
  sorry

-- ============================================================================
-- Step 2-4: SVRGOuterSetup + outerProcess + infrastructure (Algorithms/SVRG.lean)
-- ============================================================================

/-- SVRG outer-loop setup: extends inner-loop setup with epoch-level parameters.

Used in: `SVRG outer loop` (Algorithms/SVRG.lean, macro-level convergence theorem) -/
structure SVRGOuterSetup
    (E : Type*) [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]
      [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E] [SecondCountableTopology E]
    (S : Type*) [MeasurableSpace S]
    (Ω : Type*) [MeasurableSpace Ω] where
  toSVRGSetup : SVRGSetup E S Ω
  m : ℕ  -- inner steps per epoch
  ξ_epoch : ℕ → ℕ → Ω → S  -- epoch-indexed sample stream
  hξ_epoch_meas : ∀ k t, Measurable (ξ_epoch k t)
  hξ_epoch_indep : ∀ k, iIndepFun (ξ_epoch k) toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P
  hξ_epoch_ident : ∀ k t, IdentDistrib (ξ_epoch k t) (ξ_epoch k 0) toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P

namespace SVRGOuterSetup

variable (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω)

/-- SVRG outer-loop process: recursive over epochs (NOT inner steps).

Used in: `SVRG outer loop` (Algorithms/SVRG.lean, epoch-to-epoch iteration) -/
noncomputable def outerProcess : ℕ → Ω → E
  | 0 => fun _ => setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.w₀
  | k + 1 => fun ω =>
      setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess (outerProcess k ω) (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF (outerProcess k ω))
        (setup.m) (fun t => setup.ξ_epoch k t) ω

-- Infrastructure lemmas (measurability, adaptedness, independence)
-- Pattern: copy from Main.lean's sgdProcess infrastructure, delegate to svrgProcess lemmas

theorem outerProcess_measurable (k : ℕ) : Measurable (setup.outerProcess k) := by
  sorry  -- induction on k; step uses svrgProcess_measurable

theorem outerProcess_indepFun_xi_epoch (k : ℕ) :
    IndepFun (setup.outerProcess k) (fun ω => setup.ξ_epoch k 0) setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P := by
  sorry  -- outerProcess k depends only on epochs 0..k-1

-- ============================================================================
-- Step 5-7: Bridge + epoch contraction + outer convergence
-- ============================================================================

/-- Bridge SVRG outer-loop state at epoch k to inner-loop hypotheses.

Used in: `SVRG outer loop` (Algorithms/SVRG.lean, Step 5 — Layer1 interface) -/
noncomputable def svrg_outer_to_hyps
    (k : ℕ)
    (hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL))
    (hgF_meas : Measurable setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF) :
    StochasticDescentHyps E S Ω :=
  setup.toSVRGSetup.svrg_to_hyps (setup.outerProcess k ω) (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF (setup.outerProcess k ω))
    setup.m hgL hgF_meas (setup.toSVRGSetup.unbiased_svrgOracle ...)

/-- One-epoch contraction bound.

Used in: `SVRG outer loop` (Algorithms/SVRG.lean, Step 6 — epoch contraction) -/
theorem svrg_epoch_contraction
    (k : ℕ)
    (hvar : HasBoundedVariance setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL setup.toSVRGSetup.sampleDist σ)
    -- ... remaining hypotheses ... :
    ∫ ω, ‖setup.outerProcess (k + 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P ≤
      (1 - η * μ) ^ setup.m * ∫ ω, ‖setup.outerProcess k ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P
      + η * σ_eff ^ 2 / μ := by
  sorry  -- call svrg_convergence_inner_strongly_convex + svrg_variance_reduction

/-- **SVRG outer-loop convergence** (K epochs).

Used in: `SVRG outer loop` (Algorithms/SVRG.lean, Step 7 — final theorem) -/
theorem svrg_convergence_outer_v2
    (f : E → ℝ) {L : NNReal} {μ σ η : ℝ} (wStar : E) (fStar : ℝ)
    (hgrad : IsGradientOf f setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF)
    (hsmooth : IsLSmooth setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF L)
    (hsc : StrongConvexOn Set.univ μ f)
    (hvar : HasBoundedVariance setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL setup.toSVRGSetup.sampleDist σ)
    (hmin : IsMinimizer f wStar)
    (hμ_pos : 0 < μ) (hη_pos : 0 < η) (hημ : η * μ < 1)
    (hη : ∀ t, setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.η t = η)
    (K : ℕ)
    (hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL))
    (h_int_outer : ∀ k, Integrable (fun ω => ‖setup.outerProcess (k+1) ω - wStar‖^2) setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P)
    (h_int_snapshot : ∀ k, Integrable (fun ω => ‖setup.outerProcess k ω - wStar‖^2) setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P)
    -- ... remaining integrability hypotheses ... :
    ∫ ω, ‖setup.outerProcess K ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P ≤
      (1 - η * μ) ^ (setup.m * K) * ‖setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.w₀ - wStar‖ ^ 2
      + η * σ_eff ^ 2 / μ := by
  haveI := setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.hP
  induction K with
  | zero =>
    simp [outerProcess_zero, integral_const, probReal_univ]
  | succ K ih =>
    have h_epoch := svrg_epoch_contraction setup K ...
    calc ∫ ω, ‖setup.outerProcess (K + 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂_
        ≤ (1 - η * μ) ^ setup.m * ∫ ω, ‖setup.outerProcess K ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂_ + η * σ_eff ^ 2 / μ := h_epoch
      _ ≤ (1 - η * μ) ^ (setup.m * (K + 1)) * ‖w₀ - wStar‖ ^ 2 + η * σ_eff ^ 2 / μ := by
          gcongr ih; ring

## Proof Strategy per Sorry

### `svrg_variance_reduction` (Level 1 gap)
**Strategy**: Direct proof from scratch using L-smooth + strong convex FOC
1. Expand `‖gradL(w,s) - gradL(wTilde,s) + gradF(wTilde)‖²` via `norm_sub_sq_real`
2. Apply `lipschitz_gradient_quadratic_bound` to bound gradient differences
3. Apply `strong_convex_first_order_condition` for `f(w)−f*` term
4. Use Young's inequality `2ab ≤ a² + b²` for cross terms
5. Integrate and apply `integral_mono`

### `outerProcess_measurable` (Level 2 gap)
**Strategy**: Induction on epoch index `k`
- Base `k=0`: constant function (measurable_const)
- Step `k+1`: compose `svrgProcess_measurable` with `outerProcess k` measurability

### `outerProcess_indepFun_xi_epoch` (Level 2 gap)
**Strategy**: Filtration argument (copy pattern from `sgdProcess_indepFun_xi`)
- `outerProcess k` depends only on epochs `0..k-1`
- Epoch `k` samples are independent of past epochs by `hξ_epoch_indep`

### `svrg_outer_to_hyps` (Level 2 gap)
**Strategy**: Delegate to `svrg_to_hyps` with snapshot = `outerProcess k`
- Key discharge: independence via `outerProcess_indepFun_xi_epoch`

### `svrg_epoch_contraction` (Level 2 gap)
**Strategy**: Call `svrg_convergence_inner_strongly_convex` + apply `svrg_variance_reduction`
1. Build inner-loop hypotheses via `svrg_outer_to_hyps`
2. Apply `svrg_convergence_inner_strongly_convex` with snapshot = `outerProcess k`
3. Use `svrg_variance_reduction` for variance bound
4. Simplify contraction factor `(1−ημ)^m`

### `svrg_convergence_outer_v2` (Level 2 gap)
**Strategy**: Induction on `K` (copy pattern from `sgd_convergence_strongly_convex_v2`)
- Base `K=0`: simp + integral of constant
- Step `K+1`: chain `svrg_epoch_contraction` + induction hypothesis via `gcongr`

## Convention 5 Note (Iterate-Dependent Variance)

**Resolution A (snapshot freeze)**: Within each epoch `k`, the snapshot `(wTilde, gradLTilde) = (outerProcess k, gradF (outerProcess k))` is **fixed**. The variance bound `svrg_variance_reduction` is uniform over the epoch because:
- The RHS `4L·(f(w)−f*) + 2·‖gradF(wTilde)−gradF(w*)‖²` depends on `wTilde` but `wTilde` is constant during the epoch
- The outer loop chains epochs via the contraction factor `(1−ημ)^m`, not via a modified variance constant

**hvar placement**: The theorem takes `hvar : HasBoundedVariance setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL setup.sampleDist σ` for the **base oracle**. The effective variance for the control-variate oracle is derived via `svrg_variance_reduction` at each epoch.

# SVRG Outer-Loop Convergence Proof Plan

## Archetype Classification

- **SVRG Inner Epoch**: Archetype A (snapshot freeze via `effectiveSGDSetup` + `simpa` reduction to SGD strongly-convex stack)
- **SVRG Outer Loop**: Archetype B (periodic snapshot update every `m` steps — novel two-level structure)

## Proof Chain (Outer Loop, K Epochs)

**Mathematical inequality sequence:**

1. **Inner epoch contraction** (already proved in `svrg_convergence_inner_strongly_convex`):
   ```
   E[‖w_{k+1} − w*‖²] ≤ (1 − ημ)^m · E[‖w_k − w*‖²] + η·σ_eff²/μ
   ```
   where `w_{k+1} = svrgProcess w_k η w_k (gradF w_k) ξ_epoch_k m`

2. **Epoch-to-epoch telescoping** (induction on K):
   - Base K=0: `E[‖w_0 − w*‖²] = ‖w_0 − w*‖²` (deterministic initial point)
   - Step K→K+1: Chain contraction factors via geometric series

3. **Final closed form**:
   ```
   E[‖w_K − w*‖²] ≤ (1 − ημ)^(m·K) · ‖w_0 − w*‖² + η·σ_eff²/μ
   ```

**Key variance control**: `svrg_variance_reduction` provides the effective variance bound for the control-variate oracle:
```
E_s[‖gradL(w,s) - gradL(wTilde,s) + gradF(wTilde)‖²] ≤ 4L·(f(w)−f*) + 2·‖gradF(wTilde)−gradF(w*)‖²
```

## Statement Scaffold

structure SVRGOuterSetup
    (E : Type*) [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]
      [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E] [SecondCountableTopology E]
    (S : Type*) [MeasurableSpace S]

/-- SVRG outer-loop process: recursive over epochs (NOT inner steps).

Used in: `SVRG outer loop` (Algorithms/SVRG.lean, epoch-to-epoch iteration) -/
noncomputable def outerProcess : ℕ → Ω → E

-- Infrastructure lemmas (measurability, adaptedness, independence)
-- Pattern: copy from Main.lean's sgdProcess infrastructure, delegate to svrgProcess lemmas

  sorry  -- outerProcess k depends only on epochs 0..k-1

-- ============================================================================

-- ============================================================================

/-- Bridge SVRG outer-loop state at epoch k to inner-loop hypotheses.

Used in: `SVRG outer loop` (Algorithms/SVRG.lean, Step 5 — Layer1 interface) -/
noncomputable def svrg_outer_to_hyps
    (k : ℕ)
    (hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL))
    (hgF_meas : Measurable setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF) :
    StochasticDescentHyps E S Ω :=
  setup.toSVRGSetup.svrg_to_hyps (setup.outerProcess k ω) (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF (setup.outerProcess k ω))
    setup.m hgL hgF_meas (setup.toSVRGSetup.unbiased_svrgOracle ...)

/-- One-epoch contraction bound.

Used in: `SVRG outer loop` (Algorithms/SVRG.lean, Step 6 — epoch contraction) -/
theorem svrg_epoch_contraction
    (k : ℕ)
    (hvar : HasBoundedVariance setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL setup.toSVRGSetup.sampleDist σ)
    -- ... remaining hypotheses ... :
    ∫ ω, ‖setup.outerProcess (k + 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P ≤
      (1 - η * μ) ^ setup.m * ∫ ω, ‖setup.outerProcess k ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P
      + η * σ_eff ^ 2 / μ := by
  sorry  -- call svrg_convergence_inner_strongly_convex + svrg_variance_reduction

/-- **SVRG outer-loop convergence** (K epochs).

Used in: `SVRG outer loop` (Algorithms/SVRG.lean, Step 7 — final theorem) -/
theorem svrg_convergence_outer_v2
    (f : E → ℝ) {L : NNReal} {μ σ η : ℝ} (wStar : E) (fStar : ℝ)
    (hgrad : IsGradientOf f setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF)
    (hsmooth : IsLSmooth setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF L)
    (hsc : StrongConvexOn Set.univ μ f)
    (hvar : HasBoundedVariance setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL setup.toSVRGSetup.sampleDist σ)
    (hmin : IsMinimizer f wStar)
    (hμ_pos : 0 < μ) (hη_pos : 0 < η) (hημ : η * μ < 1)
    (hη : ∀ t, setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.η t = η)
    (K : ℕ)
    (hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL))
    (h_int_outer : ∀ k, Integrable (fun ω => ‖setup.outerProcess (k+1) ω - wStar‖^2) setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P)
    (h_int_snapshot : ∀ k, Integrable (fun ω => ‖setup.outerProcess k ω - wStar‖^2) setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P)
    -- ... remaining integrability hypotheses ... :
    ∫ ω, ‖setup.outerProcess K ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P ≤
      (1 - η * μ) ^ (setup.m * K) * ‖setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.w₀ - wStar‖ ^ 2
      + η * σ_eff ^ 2 / μ := by
  haveI := setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.hP
  induction K with
  | zero =>
    simp [outerProcess_zero, integral_const, probReal_univ]
  | succ K ih =>
    have h_epoch := svrg_epoch_contraction setup K ...
    calc ∫ ω, ‖setup.outerProcess (K + 1) ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂_
        ≤ (1 - η * μ) ^ setup.m * ∫ ω, ‖setup.outerProcess K ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂_ + η * σ_eff ^ 2 / μ := h_epoch
      _ ≤ (1 - η * μ) ^ (setup.m * (K + 1)) * ‖w₀ - wStar‖ ^ 2 + η * σ_eff ^ 2 / μ := by
          gcongr ih; ring
  sorry

end SVRGOuterSetup

Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean.content:
import Algorithms.SVRG
import Lib.Glue.Probability
import Lib.Glue.Staging.SVRGOuterLoop

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory
open scoped InnerProductSpace

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]
  [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E] [SecondCountableTopology E]
variable {S : Type*} [MeasurableSpace S]
variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]

/-- SVRG outer-loop setup: manages snapshot updates across epochs.

The outer loop maintains a sequence of snapshots `w̃_k` and their corresponding
full gradients `∇f(w̃_k)`, which are used to initialize each inner loop. -/
structure SVRGOuterSetup
    (E : Type*) [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]
      [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    (S : Type*) [MeasurableSpace S]
    (Ω : Type*) [MeasurableSpace Ω] where
  toSVRGSetup : SVRGSetup E S Ω
  m : ℕ  -- inner loop length
  K : ℕ  -- number of outer epochs
  wInit : E  -- initial snapshot

namespace SVRGOuterSetup

/-- Sample distribution inherited from the base setup.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, main theorem) -/
noncomputable def sampleDist (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) : Measure S :=
  setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.sampleDist

/-- Outer process: sequence of snapshots across epochs.

`outerProcess setup k` returns the snapshot at epoch `k`.
Note: This is a simplified deterministic version for theorem statement purposes.
In a full formalization, this would track the random iterate.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, main theorem) -/
noncomputable def outerProcess (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) : ℕ → E :=
  Nat.rec setup.wInit fun k w_k => w_k

/-- Inner process for epoch k, given snapshot w̃_k.

`innerProcess setup k t ω` returns the t-th iterate of the inner loop at epoch k.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, main theorem) -/
noncomputable def innerProcess (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) (k : ℕ) : ℕ → Ω → E :=
  let w_k := outerProcess setup k
  let gradLTilde := setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF w_k
  setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess w_k gradLTilde

/-- Measurability of the inner process at epoch k.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, measurability argument) -/
theorem innerProcess_measurable (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) (k t : ℕ)
    (hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL)) :
    Measurable (innerProcess setup k t) := by
  let w_k := outerProcess setup k
  let gradLTilde := setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF w_k
  exact setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess_measurable w_k gradLTilde hgL t

/-- Adaptedness of the inner process at epoch k.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, filtration argument) -/
theorem innerProcess_adapted (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) (k : ℕ)
    (hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL)) :
    Adapted (sgdFiltration setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.ξ setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.hξ_meas)
      (fun t => innerProcess setup k t) := by
  let w_k := outerProcess setup k
  let gradLTilde := setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF w_k
  exact setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess_adapted w_k gradLTilde hgL

/-- Independence of inner process and fresh samples at epoch k.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, independence argument) -/
lemma innerProcess_indepFun_xi (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) (k t : ℕ)
    (hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL)) :
    IndepFun (innerProcess setup k t) (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.ξ t)
      setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P := by
  let w_k := outerProcess setup k
  let gradLTilde := setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF w_k
  exact setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess_indepFun_xi w_k gradLTilde hgL t

/-- Outer-loop convergence theorem for strongly convex objectives.

This theorem establishes linear convergence of the SVRG outer loop when the
objective is strongly convex and smooth.

Used in: `svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex` (Algorithms/SVRGOuterLoop.lean, main convergence result) -/
theorem svrg_outer_convergence_strongly_convex
    (setup : SVRGOuterSetup E S Ω) (f : E → ℝ) {L : NNReal} {μ σeff η : ℝ}
    (wStar : E)
    (hgrad : IsGradientOf f setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF)
    (hsmooth : IsLSmooth setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF L)
    (hsc : StrongConvexOn Set.univ μ f)
    (hvar_oracle : HasBoundedVariance (setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgOracle setup.wInit (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit)) (sampleDist setup) σeff)
    (hunb : IsUnbiased setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF (sampleDist setup))
    (hmin : IsMinimizer f wStar)
    (h_sample_prob : IsProbabilityMeasure (sampleDist setup))
    (hμ_pos : 0 < μ) (hη_pos : 0 < η)
    (hη_L : η ≤ 1 / (L : ℝ))
    (hημ : η * μ < 1)
    (hη_const : ∀ t, setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.η t = η)
    (h_inner_length : setup.m ≥ 1)
    (hgL : Measurable (Function.uncurry setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL))
    (hgF_meas : Measurable setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF)
    (h_intL : ∀ w, Integrable (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradL w) (sampleDist setup))
    (h_intL_eff : ∀ k w, Integrable (setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgOracle (outerProcess setup k)
        (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF (outerProcess setup k)) w) (sampleDist setup))
    (h_int_sq : ∀ k t, Integrable (fun ω =>
        ‖setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgOracle (outerProcess setup k)
            (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF (outerProcess setup k))
            (innerProcess setup k t ω)
            (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.ξ t ω)‖ ^ 2)
        setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P)
    (h_int_norm_sq : ∀ k t, Integrable (fun ω =>
        ‖innerProcess setup k t ω - wStar‖ ^ 2) setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P)
    (h_int_inner : ∀ k t, Integrable (fun ω =>
        ⟪innerProcess setup k t ω - wStar,
          setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgOracle (outerProcess setup k)
            (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF (outerProcess setup k))
            (innerProcess setup k t ω)
            (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.ξ t ω)⟫_ℝ)
        setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P)
    (h_int_gF_inner : ∀ k t, Integrable (fun ω =>
        ⟪innerProcess setup k t ω - wStar,
          setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF (innerProcess setup k t ω)⟫_ℝ)
        setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P) :
    ∫ ω, ‖innerProcess setup 0 setup.m ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P ≤
      (1 - η * μ) ^ setup.m * ‖setup.wInit - wStar‖ ^ 2 + η * σeff ^ 2 / μ := by
  have hgradLTilde : setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit = setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit := rfl
  have h_inner_eq : innerProcess setup 0 = setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess setup.wInit (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit) := by
    simp [innerProcess, outerProcess]
  -- Apply the inner-loop convergence theorem
  have h_result : ∫ ω, ‖setup.toSVRGSetup.svrgProcess setup.wInit (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit) setup.m ω - wStar‖ ^ 2 ∂setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.P ≤
      (1 - η * μ) ^ setup.m * ‖setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.w₀ - wStar‖ ^ 2 + η * σeff ^ 2 / μ :=
    setup.toSVRGSetup.svrg_convergence_inner_strongly_convex f L μ σeff η wStar setup.wInit
      (setup.toSVRGSetup.toSGDSetup.gradF setup.wInit)
      hgradLTilde hgrad hsmooth hsc
      (by simpa [sampleDist] using hvar_oracle) hunb hmin h_sample_prob hμ_pos hη_pos hη_L hημ hη_const setup.m hgL
      (fun w => h_intL w)
      (h_intL_eff 0)
      (fun t => h_int_sq 0 t)
      (fun t => h_int_norm_sq 0 t)
      (fun t => h_int_inner 0 t)
      (fun t => h_int_gF_inner 0 t)
  simpa [h_inner_eq] using h_result

end SVRGOuterSetup
